name: Deviled Mice
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the eggs in a saucepan and cover with cold water by at least an inch.
2. With adult supervision, bring the water to a boil over high heat.
3. Turn off the heat and let the eggs simmer for 10 minutes.
4. Carefully drain the hot water from the pan into the sink.
5. Rinse the cooled eggs under cold running water.
6. Gently crack the eggshells against a hard surface, then peel them off.
7. Cut each egg in half lengthwise and scoop out the yolks into a small bowl.
8. Mash the yolks with a fork until crumbly.
9. Add mayonnaise and mix well.
10. Fill the hollowed-out egg whites with the yolk mixture.
11. Arrange lettuce leaves on a platter, leaving some aside for garnish.
12. Place the egg halves, yolk side down, on the lettuce leaves to form mouse bodies.
13. For eyes, use pimentos from olives cut into three small pieces each.
14. With an adult's help, cut 32 thin strips of green olives and join two to make a tail for each mouse.
15. Cut 32 more tiny triangular pieces from remaining olives for ears.
16. Garnish the mice by tearing lettuce leaves and placing them in front of their mouths as if they're nibbling.
17. For a finishing touch, sprinkle chocolate sprinkles on top of the platter to represent mouse droppings.
From "Gross Grub" by Cheryl Porter (Random House ISBN 0-679-86693-0), shared by Carolyn Shaw (10-95).
